framework.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system
framework.issuing_body: International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC), via ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42
framework.scope: ISO/IEC 42001 is an international management system standard for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an Artificial Intelligence Management System (AIMS). It is intended for organizations of any size and across all industries that develop, provide, or use AI-based products or services, including public sector bodies, companies, and non-profits.
framework.applies_to: Organizations of any size across all industries that develop, provide, or use AI-based products or services; applicable to public sector agencies, companies, and non-profits.
framework.penalties: Voluntary standard; no direct statutory penalties. Conformity can be certified by independent certification bodies, and nonconformity can lead to failed audits, loss or denial of certification, and contractual or customer consequences.

control.statement: The organization shall determine external and internal issues relevant to its purpose and affecting its ability to achieve the intended result(s) of its AI management system.
The organization shall determine the interested parties relevant to the AI management system and the relevant requirements of those interested parties.

Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment to the AI management system and ensure the AI policy and objectives are established and compatible with the organization’s strategic direction.
The organization shall plan actions to address AI risks and opportunities, integrating and implementing those actions into the AI management system processes.
AI objectives shall be established at relevant functions and levels and be monitored, communicated, and updated as appropriate.
The organization shall determine the necessary competence of persons doing work under its control that affects AI management system performance and ensure competent persons are assigned.
The organization shall perform AI risk assessment at planned intervals and when significant changes are proposed or occur, and retain documented information of the results.
The organization shall implement AI risk treatment, determine how treatment will be performed, and retain documented information on the treatment results.
The organization shall determine what needs to be monitored and measured, the methods for monitoring and measurement, and when the results shall be analyzed and evaluated.
The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the AI management system.

source.title: AI management systems: What businesses need to know
